title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade lunch. Get step-by-step instructions!
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with rice and naan, garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani), a beloved Indian classic!

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian curry featuring tender chicken pieces swimming in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This iconic dish is celebrated for its harmonious blend of mild spices, butter, and a hint of sweetness, making it a comforting and flavorful experience. It is a perfect choice for an indulgent lunch, offering a taste of authentic Indian culinary tradition.

ingredients.notes: from 1/4 cup cashews soaked in warm water then blended

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 teaspoon ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chili powder, garam masala, cumin powder, coriander powder, and a pinch of salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 1 tablespoon of butter and the remaining 2 teaspoons of ginger-garlic paste. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the pureed tomatoes and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring frequently, until the mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Cashew Paste
instructions.text: Add the remaining red chili powder, garam masala, and salt to the tomato-onion mixture. Cook for 1-2 minutes. Stir in the cashew paste and 1/2 cup of water. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. For a smoother sauce, you can blend this mixture using an immersion blender or cool and blend in a regular blender, then pass through a sieve.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish the Curry
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the sauce. Add the heavy cream, 1 tablespoon of butter, and sugar. Mix gently to combine. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes on low heat, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the sauce. Ensure the sauce does not boil vigorously after adding cream.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Crush the kasoori methi between your palms and sprinkle it over the butter chicken. Stir gently. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and an extra dab of butter if desired. Serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

tips: For an extra smooth sauce, blend the tomato-onion-cashew mixture after cooking and before adding the chicken, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Adjust the spice level to your preference; for more heat, add extra red chili powder or a finely chopped green chili. For a milder taste, reduce the chili powder.
Always add cream and kasoori methi at the end of cooking and simmer gently to prevent curdling and preserve their delicate flavors. Do not boil vigorously after adding cream.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: Your Guide to a Quintessential Indian Lunch

Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Butter Chicken, known in India as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian lunch recipe has transcended borders, becoming a global favorite for its unparalleled richness, creamy texture, and perfectly balanced flavors. Imagine tender pieces of chicken swimming in a velvety tomato-based sauce, subtly spiced and finished with a generous swirl of butter and cream. It’s a dish that promises comfort, indulgence, and a truly authentic taste of India. Whether you’re a seasoned cook or a culinary novice, mastering this classic is a rewarding experience that will elevate your home cooking to new heights.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Its origins trace back to the 1950s in Delhi, India, at the Moti Mahal restaurant. The story goes that leftover tandoori chicken pieces were combined with a rich, tomato-based gravy, butter, and cream to prevent them from drying out. The result was an instant sensation that quickly became a staple in Indian cuisine. At its core, Murgh Makhani is characterized by its distinctively mild yet complex flavor profile. The sauce is typically made from tomatoes, cashews (for thickness and richness), ginger, garlic, and a blend of aromatic spices like garam masala, cumin, coriander, and fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). The chicken is often marinated in yogurt and spices before being grilled or pan-fried, adding depth and a smoky char that complements the creamy sauce beautifully. This beloved Butter Chicken dish is the epitome of comfort food, embodying the warmth and hospitality of Indian cooking.

## Ingredients You Need

To create an authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, gathering the right ingredients is key. Here's a breakdown of what you'll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, or regular for heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 green chili, slit (optional, for heat)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 (400g) can crushed tomatoes or 3-4 medium fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup cashews, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1 tablespoon sugar (to balance acidity)
*   1 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   Salt to taste

Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of heavy cream

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making delicious Butter Chicken at home is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for a perfect meal:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, without overcrowding the pan. Cook for 3-5 minutes per side until lightly charred and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Add Aromatics and Spices:** Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and green chili (if using), and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the red chili powder, ground coriander, turmeric powder, and garam masala. Sauté for 30 seconds, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
5.  **Build the Sauce:** Pour in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh tomato puree) and a pinch of salt. Add the soaked and drained cashews. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have cooked down and the oil starts to separate.
6.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, you can strain it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.
7.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces, water or chicken broth, and sugar. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce.
8.  **Final Touches:** Stir in the crushed kasoori methi and heavy cream. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, just until heated through, but do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt if needed.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home involves a few insider tricks:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The yogurt and spices tenderize the chicken and infuse it with flavor, making a significant difference in the final taste of your Butter Chicken.
*   **Char the Chicken:** If possible, grill the marinated chicken in a tandoor oven or on a barbecue for an authentic smoky flavor. If not, cooking it in a hot pan until it develops a slight char will mimic this effect beautifully.
*   **Blend Until Smooth:** A truly authentic Murgh Makhani sauce is incredibly smooth. Blending the sauce base thoroughly, and even straining it, will give you that desirable silky texture.
*   **Don't Forget Kasoori Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable for authentic flavor. Crushing them between your palms before adding them releases their aromatic oils.
*   **Balance with Sugar:** Tomatoes can be quite acidic. A little sugar helps balance the flavors, bringing out the sweetness and richness of the dish without making it overtly sweet.
*   **Good Quality Ingredients:** Use fresh, high-quality spices, good butter, and heavy cream for the best possible flavor and texture.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a complete meal in itself, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments.
*   **Naan Bread:** Warm, fluffy naan bread (garlic naan or butter naan are excellent choices) is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich, creamy sauce.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or a fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast in texture and helps to soak up the luxurious gravy.
*   **Raita:** A cooling cucumber raita (yogurt dip) or a simple green salad can cut through the richness of the Butter Chicken, offering a refreshing counterpoint.
*   **Pickles and Chutneys:** A side of mango pickle or a sweet chutney can add an extra layer of flavor and zest to your meal.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is perfect as is, you can easily adapt this recipe to suit dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Marinate and cook the paneer or tofu similarly to the chicken, or add the vegetables directly to the simmering sauce.
*   **Dairy-Free Option:** Use coconut cream instead of heavy cream and a dairy-free butter alternative. Ensure your yogurt for marinating is also dairy-free. Cashews still provide the necessary richness.
*   **Spicier Version:** Increase the amount of red chili powder or add more green chilies to the sauce for a spicier kick.
*   **Healthier Twist:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or use light cream. While it won't be as rich, it will still be delicious. You can also opt for lean chicken breast.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A quick trick for a smoky flavor without a tandoor is to place a small, hot piece of charcoal in a small metal bowl in the center of your finished Butter Chicken, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for 5-10 minutes.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a recipe; it's an experience. Its unparalleled taste and comforting warmth make it a perfect choice for a special Indian lunch or a cozy dinner. With this detailed guide, you're well on your way to creating a truly memorable and authentic dish that will impress everyone at your table. Enjoy the journey of bringing this Indian culinary masterpiece to life in your own kitchen!
